title: thelypteris auriculifera
intro: Thelypteris auriculifera, also known as the golden-eared maiden fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Thelypteris auriculifera include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antioxidant, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared by drying and then making a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various ailments, including coughs and colds.
